key_points.text: Compute Networking segment revenue reached $74.55 billion in Q1 FY2027, up 88% year-over-year, driven by Blackwell systems ramp and strong demand for InfiniBand and NVLink solutions
Data Center revenue reached $71.5 billion in fiscal year 2026, reflecting dominant market position with NVIDIA powering over 78% of supercomputers on the global TOP500 list

citation.quote: NVIDIA powers over 78% of the supercomputers on the global TOP500 list, including 9 of the top 10 systems on the Green500 list

key_points.text: Gross margin improved significantly in Q1 FY2027 on lower inventory provisions, with the prior year's $4.5 billion H20 excess inventory charge not recurring

citation.quote: Gross margin increased from a year ago on lower inventory provisions, primarily due to the prior year's $4.5 billion charge associated with H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations

key_points.text: Total company revenue for fiscal year 2026 was $215.938 billion with net income of $120.067 billion, reflecting strong profitability
Blackwell architecture is driving revenue growth across multiple customer segments including cloud providers, AI model makers, and enterprises adopting generative and agentic AI applications

citation.quote: In 2024, we launched the NVIDIA Blackwell architecture – connecting 36 Grace CPUs and 72 Blackwell GPUs in a data center scale, liquid-cooled design – for real-time trillion-parameter inference and training

key_points.text: Graphics segment operating income increased 79% year-over-year in Q1 FY2027, driven by Blackwell architecture sales
Edge Computing revenue reached $6.4 billion in Q1 FY2027, up 29% year-over-year, demonstrating expansion beyond traditional data center markets

citation.quote: Edge Computing revenue for the first quarter was $6.4 billion, up 29% from a year ago and up 10% sequentially
